Subsection.Text: Section 8300 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
The Minister may waive in writing the application of the definition individual pension plan in subsection (1) if is just and equitable to do so having regard to all the circumstances.
Subsections (1) and (2) are deemed to have come into force on March 23, 2011.
The portion of subsection 8303(6) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsections (3) and 8304(5), (7) and (10), and subject to subsection (6.1) and paragraph 8304(2)(h), the amount of an individual’s qualifying transfers made in connection with a past service event is the total of all amounts each of which is
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on March 23, 2011.
The portion of subsection 8304(5) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (10), if
Section 8304 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (9):

If there is a past service event in relation to a defined benefit provision under an individual pension plan, the provisional PSPA of an individual with respect to an employer that is associated with the past service event is the amount, if any, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the greater of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the provisional PSPA that would be determined if

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: this subsection did not apply,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the value of C in subsection 8303(3) were nil, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the value of D in subsection 8304(5) were nil, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the proportion of the fair market value of all property held in connection with the individual’s designated savings arrangements at the time of the past service event, that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the duration (measured in years, including any fraction of a year) of a period that is pensionable service of the individual under the provision

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the lesser of 35 and the number of years by which the individual’s age in whole years at the time of the past service event exceeds 18, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the individual’s unused RRSP deduction room at the end of the year immediately preceding the calendar year that includes the past service event, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the actuarial liabilities of the retirement benefits associated with the past service event, determined on the basis of the funding assumptions specified under subsections 8515(6) and (7), at the same effective date as the actuarial valuation that forms the basis for the recommendation referred to in subsection 147.2(2) of the Act that is not earlier than the calendar year of the past service event; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the individual’s qualifying transfers made in connection with the past service event.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (10) does not apply to a past service event in relation to an individual pension plan if the provisional PSPA of the member determined under subsections 8303(3) and 8304(5) would be nil if no qualifying transfers were made in connection with the past service event, unless it is a past service event that results from the establishment of the plan or from an amendment to the plan to provide additional retirement benefits.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to past service events occurring after March 22, 2011.
Subsection 8500(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: IPP minimum amount, for a year, for a person who is a member of an individual pension plan (or a beneficiary, in respect of the plan, who was, at the time of the member’s death, a spouse or common-law partner of the member) means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if there is only one such person in respect of the plan, the minimum amount that would be determined under subsection 146.3(1) of the Act for the year in respect of the plan if the plan were a registered retirement income fund that held the same property as the property held by the plan and the person were the annuitant of the fund, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the minimum amount that would be determined under subsection 146.3(1) of the Act if the person were the annuitant of a registered retirement income fund and the fair market value of the property held in connection with the fund at the beginning of the year were determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of all property held in connection with the plan at the beginning of the year,
is the amount of the actuarial liabilities in respect of the benefits payable to the person under the terms of the plan at the beginning of the year, and
is the amount of the actuarial liabilities in respect of all benefits payable under the terms of the plan at the beginning of the year; (minimum RRI)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2012 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 8501(1)(e) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: there is no reason to expect that the plan may become a revocable plan under subsection 147.1(8) or (9) of the Act or subsections 8503(15) or (26) or 8506(4).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2012 and subsequent taxation years.

Paragraph 8502(d) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (viii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (ix) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ix):

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of the IPP minimum amount for an individual that is not described in subparagraph (i).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2012 and subsequent taxation years.
Section 8503 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (25):
An individual pension plan becomes a revocable plan at the end of a year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is a member or a beneficiary, in respect of the plan, who was, at the time of the member’s death, a spouse or common-law partner of the member, is in receipt of retirement benefits under the terms of the plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person has attained 71 years of age before the year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the plan has not paid in the year an amount to the person equal to the greater of the retirement benefits payable to the person for the year and the IPP minimum amount for the person for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2012 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 8517(3) of the Regulations and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Subsection (3.01) applies in respect of a transfer of an amount on behalf of an individual in full or partial satisfaction of the individual’s entitlement to benefits under a defined benefit provision of a registered pension plan if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual is an employee or a former employee of an employer (or a predecessor employer of the employer);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the employer

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was a participating employer under the provision,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is the subject of proceedings commenced under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act or the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: has ceased making regular contributions under the provision;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: after the commencement of the proceedings, lifetime retirement benefits paid or payable to the individual under the provision have been reduced because the assets of the plan are insufficient to pay the benefits provided under the provision of the plan as registered;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the plan is not a designated plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has approved the application of subsection (3.01) in respect of the transfer.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies, the description of A in subsection (1) shall be read as follows in respect of the transfer:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the individual’s lifetime retirement benefits under the provision commuted in connection with the transfer, as determined under subsection (4), but without reference to the benefit reduction referred to in paragraph (3)(c); and

Subsection.Text: If a particular amount is transferred in full or partial satisfaction of an individual’s entitlement to benefits under a defined benefit provision of a registered pension plan and subsection (3.01) had applied in respect of a transfer (in this subsection referred to as the “initial transfer”) of an amount on behalf of the individual under the provision, for the purpose of paragraph 147.3(4)(c) of the Act the prescribed amount in respect of the transfer of the particular amount is the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular amount, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which the prescribed amount in respect of the initial transfer exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a previous transfer to which this subsection or subsection (3.01) applied in respect of the individual’s entitlement to benefits under the provision.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after 2010.
The heading “PRESCRIBED PROGRAMS OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY” before section 9400 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) applies to the 2011 and subsequent taxation years.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 9400:

Heading.TitleText: Program of Artistic, Cultural, Recreational or Developmental Activity

Section.MarginalNote: Definition of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity

Subsection.Text: In this section, artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity means a supervised activity, including an activity adapted for children in respect of whom an amount is deductible under section 118.3 of the Act, suitable for children (other than a physical activity), that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is intended to contribute to a child’s ability to develop creative skills or expertise, acquire and apply knowledge, or improve dexterity or coordination, in an artistic or cultural discipline including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: literary arts,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: visual arts,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: performing arts,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: music,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: media,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: customs, and

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: heritage;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provides a substantial focus on wilderness and the natural environment;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: assists with the development and use of intellectual skills;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: includes structured interaction among children where supervisors teach or assist children to develop interpersonal skills; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: provides enrichment or tutoring in academic subjects.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prescribed program of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the definition eligible expense in subsection 118.031(1) of the Act, a prescribed program of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a weekly program, that is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of eight or more consecutive weeks in which all or substantially all the activities include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a program, that is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of five or more consecutive days of which more than 50% of the daily activities include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a program, that is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of eight or more consecutive weeks, offered to children by a club, association or similar organization (in this section referred to as an “organization”) in circumstances where a participant in the program may select amongst a variety of activities if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 50% of those activities offered to children by the organization are activities that include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: more than 50% of the time scheduled for activities offered to children in the program is scheduled for activities that include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a membership in an organization, that is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of eight or more consecutive weeks if more than 50% of all the activities offered to children by the organization include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the definition eligible expense in subsection 118.031(1) of the Act, a prescribed program of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity is that portion of a program, which program does not meet the requirements of paragraph (2)(c) and is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of eight or more consecutive weeks, offered to children by an organization in circumstances where a participant in the program may select amongst a variety of activities

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is the percentage of those activities offered to children by the organization that are activities that include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is the percentage of the time scheduled for activities in the program that is scheduled for activities that include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the definition eligible expense in subsection 118.031(1) of the Act, a prescribed program of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity is that portion of a membership in an organization, which membership does not meet the requirements of paragraph (2)(d) and is not part of a school’s curriculum, of a duration of eight or more consecutive weeks that is the percentage of all the activities offered to children by the organization that are activities that include a significant amount of artistic, cultural, recreational or developmental activity.
Subsection (1) applies to the 2011 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of subparagraph (c)(iii) of Class 29 in Schedule II to the Regulations before clause (A) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: after March 18, 2007 and before 2014 if the property is machinery, or equipment,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after 2011.
The portion of paragraph (c) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the French version of the Regulations before clause (i)(A) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: qui, selon le cas :

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: font partie d’un système, sauf un système à cycles combinés amélioré, qui, à la fois :

Subsection.Text: Clause (c)(ii)(A) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, to generate electrical energy using only a combination of natural gas and thermal waste from one or more natural gas compressor systems located on a natural gas pipeline,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of Class 43.1 in Schedule II to the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (i), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: equipment that is used by the taxpayer, or by a lessee of the taxpayer, to generate electrical energy in a process all or substantially all of the energy input of which is thermal waste, other than

Provision.Label: (A)
Provision.Text: equipment that uses heat produced by a gas turbine that is part of the first stage of a combined cycle system, and

Provision.Label: (B)
Provision.Text: equipment that, on the date of its acquisition, uses chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) or hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs), within the meaning assigned by the Ozone-Depleting Substances Regulations, 1998,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to property acquired on or after March 22, 2011.
Subparagraph 16(1)(a)(ii) of the Canada Education Savings Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a parent of a beneficiary under the receiving RESP was a parent of an individual who was, immediately before the transfer, a beneficiary under the transferring RESP and

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the receiving RESP is an RESP that allows more than one beneficiary at any one time, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in any other case, the beneficiary under the receiving RESP had not attained 21 years of age at the time the receiving RESP was entered into;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of property transferred after 2010.

Section.Text: If Bill C-10, introduced in the 1st session of the 41st Parliament and entitled the Safe Streets and Communities Act, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 116 of that Act and subsection 52(2) of this Act are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (a) of the definition ineligible individual in subsection 149.1(1) of the Income Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: convicted of a relevant criminal offence unless it is a conviction for which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a pardon has been granted and the pardon has not been revoked or ceased to have effect, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a record suspension has been ordered under the Criminal Records Act and the record suspension has not been revoked or ceased to have effect,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 149.1 of the Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deeming rule — Safe Streets and Communities Act
Subsection.Text: In this section, a reference to a record suspension is deemed also to be a reference to a pardon that is granted or issued under the Criminal Records Act.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: SOFTWOOD LUMBER PRODUCTS EXPORT CHARGE ACT, 2006

Section.Text: Subsection 12(1) of the Softwood Lumber Products Export Charge Act, 2006 is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a softwood lumber product is exported from a region in a particular month, the amount of the charge in respect of that export is the amount calculated by applying the rate applicable for the month under this Act to the export price of the product as determined in accordance with section 13.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 12.1:

The rate of charge applicable in respect of an export of a softwood lumber product from Ontario or Quebec on or after the day on which this section comes into force is equal to the sum of the rate of charge otherwise applicable under this Act and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 0.1%, in the case of an export from Ontario; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 2.6%, in the case of an export from Quebec.

Section.Text: Subsection 14(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Surge mechanism if certain provisions apply
Subsection.Text: If the rate of charge provided for by section 12.1 or 12.2 applies in respect of an export, the increase under subsection (1) in respect of that export is to be calculated as if the rate had not applied and the rate provided for by subsection 12(3) or (4) had applied.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister for International Trade, by regulation, conditionally or unconditionally, exempt the export of softwood lumber products from a region from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the charge referred to in section 10; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the application of any part of a rate of charge that is higher than the rate of charge provided for by subsection 12(3) or (4).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 40(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Refund calculated without regard to certain provisions
Subsection.Text: The amount of a refund under this section is to be determined using the amount of a charge that is calculated without regard to sections 12.1 and 12.2.
Subsection 40(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A refund in respect of an amount shall not be paid under subsection (1) to a person unless the person files, in the prescribed manner, an application for the refund in the prescribed form and containing prescribed information within four years after the day on which the amount was paid by the person.

Section.Text: Section 41 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (3), the Minister may at any time extend, in writing, the time for filing an application for a refund for the payment of the increase of the amount of the charge referred to in subsection 14(1).

Section.Text: This Part is deemed to have come into force on March 1, 2011.
Section 5 of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this Act, goods are imported from one of the following countries if they are shipped directly to Canada from that country:
Section 13 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, by order, amend the List of Tariff Provisions to change a tariff item number or a description of goods in a tariff item, or to add, repeal or replace a tariff item, if the amendment does not affect the rate of customs duty applicable to those goods.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 14(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to reduce a rate of customs duty on goods imported from a country, and to make consequential amendments,
Paragraph 14(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: by way of compensation for any action taken under any of the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 55(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 60,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 63(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 69(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 70(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 71(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 71.01(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 71.1(2),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 71.5(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 5(3), (3.2) or (4.1) of the Export and Import Permits Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, amend the List of Countries to reflect a change in the name of a country set out in that List.

Section.Text: Paragraph 24(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the goods are entitled to that tariff treatment in accordance with regulations made under section 16 or an order made under any of the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 31(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 34(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 38(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 42(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 45(13),

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 48,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 49.01(8),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 49.2,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 49.5(8).

Section.Text: Section 27 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The following abbreviations, as defined below, apply in the schedule.

Definition.Text: AUT refers to the Australia Tariff.
CCCT refers to the Commonwealth Caribbean Countries Tariff.
CIAT refers to the Canada–Israel Agreement Tariff.
COLT refers to the Colombia Tariff.
CRT refers to the Costa Rica Tariff.
CT refers to the Chile Tariff.
GPT refers to the General Preferential Tariff.
IT refers to the Iceland Tariff.
LDCT refers to the Least Developed Country Tariff.
MFN refers to the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff.
MT refers to the Mexico Tariff.
MUST refers to the Mexico–United States Tariff.
NT refers to the Norway Tariff.
NZT refers to the New Zealand Tariff.
PT refers to the Peru Tariff.
SLT refers to the Switzerland–Liechtenstein Tariff.
UST refers to the United States Tariff.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 31(1)(a) and (b) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: accorder le bénéfice du tarif de la nation la plus favorisée à tout ou partie des marchandises originaires d’un pays assujetti au tarif général;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: retirer le bénéfice du tarif de la nation la plus favorisée à tout ou partie des marchandises originaires d’un pays bénéficiaire de ce tarif et les assujettir au tarif général;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 31(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: indicate, to the extent required, the tariff treatment of the country to which the order applies.

Section.Text: Subsection 34(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff to any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, that country is a developing country;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: withdraw entitlement to the General Preferential Tariff from any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of that Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reduce a rate of customs duty set out following the abbreviation “GPT” in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions and the “F” Staging List.

Section.Text: Subsection 38(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend entitlement to the Least Developed Country Tariff to any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of the General Preferential Tariff, if, in the opinion of the Governor in Council, that country is a least developed country;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: withdraw entitlement to the Least Developed Country Tariff from any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of that Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reduce a rate of customs duty set out following the abbreviation “LDCT” in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions and the “F” Staging List.

Section.Text: Subsection 42(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: extend entitlement to the Commonwealth Caribbean Countries Tariff to any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of that Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: withdraw entitlement to the Commonwealth Caribbean Countries Tariff from any goods that originate in a country that is a beneficiary of that Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: reduce a rate of customs duty set out following the abbreviation “CCCT” in the column entitled “Preferential Tariff” in the List of Tariff Provisions and the “F” Staging List.

Section.Text: Section 47 of the Act is repealed.
Section 49 of the Act is repealed.
Section 49.3 of the Act is repealed.
Section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions in this section apply in sections 55 to 67.

Definition.Text: contribute importantly, in respect of goods imported from one of the following countries, means to be an important cause, but not necessarily the most important cause:
principal cause, in respect of goods imported from one of the following countries, means an important cause that is no less important than any other cause:
surge, in respect of goods imported from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a NAFTA country, has the meaning given that word by Article 805 of the North American Free Trade Agreement; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Chile, has the meaning given that word by Article F-05 of the Canada–Chile Free Trade Agreement.

Section.Text: Section 57 of the Act is replaced by the following:
No order may be made under subsection 55(1), on the basis of a report of the Minister, with respect to any prescribed agricultural goods that may be subject to a surtax under subsection 68(1).
Section 59.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order made under subsection 55(1) may exclude goods of any kind imported from one of the following countries if it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, on the basis of a report under section 20 or 29 of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act, that the quantity of those goods being imported is not a principal cause of serious injury or threat of serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods:
Subsection 63(4.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection (1) may exclude goods of any kind imported from one of the following countries if it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, on the basis of a report under the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act, that the quantity of those goods being imported is not a principal cause of serious injury or threat of serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods:

Section.Text: The heading before section 72 and sections 72 to 77 of the Act are repealed.
Section 79 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order made under any of the following provisions may provide that goods that are in transit to Canada at the time the order comes into force are entitled to the tariff treatment that was applicable to those goods immediately before that time:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 53(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 55(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 60;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 63(1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 69(2);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 70(2);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 71.01(1);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 71.1(2);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 71.5(1).

Section.Text: The definition customs duties in section 80 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: customs duties, other than for the purposes of sections 95 and 96, means customs duties imposed under Part 2, other than surtaxes or temporary duties imposed under Division 4 of Part 2.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 94(1)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: additional customs duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: surtaxes or temporary duties imposed under Division 4 of that Part.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 99(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, relief of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or under the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax or temporary duty imposed under Division 4 of Part 2, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty imposed under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted,

Section.Text: Paragraph 113(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the circumstances in which, and the classes of goods in respect of which, a refund or drawback of duties levied under sections 21.1 to 21.3 or under the Special Import Measures Act, a surtax or temporary duty imposed under Division 4 of Part 2, a tax levied under the Excise Tax Act or a duty levied under the Excise Act, 2001 may not be granted under subsection (1);

Section.Text: Paragraphs 133(j) and (j.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of tariff item No. 9971.00.00, prescribing conditions under which goods that have been exported to one of the following countries for repair or alteration may be imported:

Paragraph.Label: (j.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of tariff item No. 9992.00.00, prescribing conditions under which goods that have been exported to one of the following countries for repair or alteration may be imported:

Section.Text: Sections 137 to 139 of the Act are replaced by the following:
In sections 140 and 143 to 146, former Act means the Customs Tariff as it read immediately before the coming into force of section 214.
Sections 141 and 142 of the Act are repealed.
The List of Countries and Applicable Tariff Treatments set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the List of Countries and Applicable Tariff Treatments set out in Schedule 1 to this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9971.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: replacing the reference beginning with “All the foregoing,” and ending with “in that country.” with a reference to “All the foregoing, regardless of the country of origin or tariff treatment, returned to Canada after having been exported to one of the countries listed below for repair or alteration in that country.”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adding the following before Note 1:

Subsection.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9990.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: replacing the reference to “Commercial samples imported from the United States, Mexico, Chile, Costa Rica, Peru or Colombia,” with a reference to “Commercial samples imported from one of the countries listed below,”;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: replacing, in paragraph (i), the reference to “in Canadian, Chilean, Mexican, Costa Rican, Peruvian or Colombian currency,” with a reference to “in Canadian currency or the currency of the country from which it was imported”; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: adding, at the end of that Description of Goods, the following:

Subsection.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item No. 9992.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: Goods, regardless of the country of origin or tariff treatment, other than the goods of tariff item No. 9971.00.00, returned to Canada after having been exported to one of the countries listed below for repair or alteration in that country.

Section.Text: The Description of Goods of tariff item 9897.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing the reference to “mattresses imported under tariff item No. 9806.00.00, 9807.00.00, 9808.00.00, 9809.00.00 or 9810.00.00” with a reference to “mattresses imported under tariff item No. 9805.00.00, 9806.00.00, 9807.00.00, 9808.00.00, 9809.00.00 or 9810.00.00”.
Tariff item Nos. 9916.00.00, 9923.00.00, 9925.00.00, 9928.00.00, 9929.00.00, 9930.00.00 and 9946.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act are repealed.

The Description of Goods of Tariff item No. 9969.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by striking out the reference to “, other than fixed pitch solid propellers of a diameter exceeding 23 cm but not exceeding 6.5 m and controllable pitch propeller blades,”.

Section.Text: The List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in numerical order, the tariff provisions set out in Schedule 2 to this Act.
The following tariff items in the List of Intermediate and Final Rates for the Tariff Items of the “F” Staging Category set out in the schedule to the Act are repealed:
Sections 137 and 142 to 144 come into force on January 1, 2012.
The List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Customs Tariff is amended by adding, in numerical order, the tariff provisions set out in Schedule 3 to this Act.
This Part comes into force on January 1, 2012.

Subsection.Text: Section 5 of the Canada Education Savings Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

If, in a month following January in a particular year, an individual who was not the primary caregiver of a beneficiary in January of that year becomes the beneficiary’s primary caregiver, then the adjusted income to be used for the purposes of subsection (4) in respect of contributions made to the trustee of the trust designated by that individual is the adjusted income used to determine the amount of a child tax benefit for the first month in the particular year in respect of which the individual’s eligibility for the child tax benefit has been established.

In applying subsection (6.1) in respect of a beneficiary for whom the individual becomes the beneficiary’s primary caregiver in December, the reference to “the first month in the particular year in respect of which the individual’s eligibility for the child tax benefit has been established” in that subsection is to be read as a reference to “January of the next year”.
Subsection 5(7) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

La somme visée au paragraphe (4) est versée au fiduciaire de toute fiducie que le responsable du bénéficiaire au moment où la cotisation est versée désigne en la forme et selon les modalités que le ministre approuve.
Section 5 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

If there is more than one primary caregiver of the beneficiary at the time a contribution is made, the amount referred to in subsection (4) is to be paid to the trustee of the trust designated under subsection (7) to which a contribution is first made.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Additional grant less than maximum amount
Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, if there is more than one primary caregiver of the beneficiary and the total of all amounts paid under subsection (4) to the trustee of the trust to which a contribution is first made is less than the maximum amount that may be paid under that subsection, then amounts may be paid under that subsection to the trustee of a trust designated by any primary caregiver of the beneficiary.

Section.Text: This Part is deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2011.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 3(1)(a) of the Children’s Special Allowances Act after subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: and who resides in an institution, a group foster home, the private home of foster parents or in the private home of a guardian, tutor or other individual occupying a similar role for the month, under a decree, order or judgment of a competent tribunal; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(1)(a) of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), applies to special allowances payable for months after December 2011.
Section 9 of the Children’s Special Allowance Regulations is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: For the purposes of the Act, a child is considered to be maintained by an applicant in a month if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant, at the end of the month, provides for the child’s care, maintenance, education, training and advancement to a greater extent than any other department, agency or institution or any person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the applicant is an entity referred to in paragraph 3(1)(a) or (b) of the Act that has applied in respect of a child who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was formerly in the care of foster parents or was formerly maintained by an entity referred to in paragraph 3(1)(a) or (b) of the Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: has been placed in the permanent or temporary custody of a guardian, tutor or other individual occupying a similar role for the month, under a decree, order or judgment of a competent tribunal who has received financial assistance from the applicant for the month in respect of the child’s maintenance.

Subsection.Text: Section 9 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to special allowances payable for months after December 2011.

Section.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Canada Student Financial Assistance Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In this Act, the words and expressions borrower, consolidated student loan agreement, course, family income, family physician, financial assistance, full-time student, loan year, nurse, nurse practitioner, part-time student, period of studies, post-secondary school level, program of studies, severe permanent disability, student loan, student loan agreement and under-served rural or remote community have the meanings assigned by the regulations.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 9.1:

Heading.TitleText: FAMILY PHYSICIANS, NURSES AND NURSE PRACTITIONERS IN UNDER-SERVED RURAL OR REMOTE COMMUNITIES

Subsection.Text: The Minister may forgive an amount in respect of a student loan to a borrower who begins to work in an under-served rural or remote community as a family physician, nurse or nurse practitioner, if the borrower meets the prescribed conditions.
The forgiveness takes effect on the prescribed day.
For the purposes of subsection (1), the Minister may enter into an agreement with a lender for the purchase of a student loan made by the lender.

Section.Text: Section 13 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The aggregate amount of student loans made under this Act that are outstanding may not exceed the prescribed amount.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 15(1)(f.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an amount on account of principal or interest is not required to be paid in respect of student loans;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 15(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (o):

Paragraph.Label: (o.1)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the amount that may be forgiven in respect of a year under section 9.2 in respect of a student loan;

Paragraph.Label: (o.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the maximum number of years in respect of which amounts may be forgiven under section 9.2 in respect of a student loan;

Subsection.Text: Section 15 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum aggregate amount of outstanding student loans
Subsection.Text: On the Minister’s recommendation with the Minister of Finance’s concurrence, the Governor in Council may make regulations, for the purposes of section 13,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the aggregate amount of outstanding student loans that may not be exceeded; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the student loans that are to be considered for the purposes of determining, at a given time, the aggregate amount of outstanding student loans.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 17.1(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: deny the person financial assistance for a prescribed period;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 17.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may not take any measure under subsection (1) or (2) later than six years after the day on which he or she becomes aware of the false statement or misrepresentation or the false or misleading information.

Section.Text: The Canada Student Loans Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Heading.TitleText: FAMILY PHYSICIANS, NURSES AND NURSE PRACTITIONERS IN UNDER-SERVED RURAL OR REMOTE COMMUNITIES

Subsection.Text: The Minister may forgive an amount in respect of a guaranteed student loan to a borrower who begins to work in an under-served rural or remote community as a family physician, nurse or nurse practitioner, if the borrower meets the prescribed conditions.
The forgiveness takes effect on the prescribed day.
For the purposes of subsection (1), the Minister may enter into an agreement with a lender for the purchase of a guaranteed student loan made by the lender.
Paragraph 17(k.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the circumstances in which an amount on account of principal or interest is not required to be paid in respect of guaranteed student loans;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 17(r) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (q.2)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the amount that may be forgiven in respect of a year under section 11.1 in respect of a guaranteed student loan;

Paragraph.Label: (q.3)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the maximum number of years in respect of which amounts may be forgiven under section 11.1 in respect of a guaranteed student loan;

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: defining the expressions family physician, full-time student, nurse, nurse practitioner, part-time student, responsible officer of a lender and under-served rural or remote community for the purposes of this Act;

Subsection.Text: Sections 152 and 153, subsections 155(1) and (2) and sections 157 and 158 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 154 and subsection 155(3) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 96 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8.6):
If an employer’s premium is $10,000 or less for 2010, the Minister shall refund to the employer a portion of the premium for 2011 determined by the following formula if that amount is more than $2:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the employer’s premium in 2010; and
is the amount of the employer’s premium in 2011.

Subsection.MarginalNote: P1 can be equal to zero
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (8.7), P1 is equal to zero if a person was not required to pay an employer’s premium in 2010.
A refund under subsection (8.7) shall not exceed $1,000.
Section 96 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (13):
Despite subsection (13), no interest shall be paid on refunds payable under subsection (8.7).

Heading.Label: PART 9
Heading.TitleText: GAS TAX FUND — FINANCING MUNICIPAL INFRASTRUCTURE

Section.Text: On the requisition of the Minister set out in Schedule I.1 of the Financial Administration Act with respect to the Office of Infrastructure of Canada, there may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund for each fiscal year beginning on or after April 1, 2014, in accordance with the terms and conditions approved by the Treasury Board, a sum of not more than $2,000,000,000 to provinces, territories, municipalities, municipal associations, provincial, territorial and municipal entities and First Nations for the purpose of municipal, regional and First Nations infrastructure.

Heading.Label: PART 10
Heading.TitleText: CANADIAN SECURITIES REGULATION REGIME TRANSITION OFFICE ACT

Section.Text: Subsection 14(1) of the Canadian Securities Regulation Regime Transition Office Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make direct payments, in an aggregate amount not exceeding $33,000,000, or any other amount that may be specified in an appropriation Act, to the Transition Office for its use.

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition eligible wages in subsection 2(1) of the Wage Earner Protection Program Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: wages other than severance pay and termination pay that were earned during the longer of the following periods:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the six-month period ending on the first day on which there was a receiver in relation to the former employer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period beginning on the day that is six months before the day on which a proposal under Division I of Part III of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act is filed by or in respect of the employer or the day on which proceedings under the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act are commenced and ending on the date of the bankruptcy or the first day on which there was a receiver in relation to the former employer; and

Section.Text: The Wage Earner Protection Program Act, as amended by section 163, applies

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of wages owing to an individual by an employer who becomes bankrupt after June 5, 2011; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of wages owing to an individual by an employer any of whose property comes under the possession or control of a receiver as defined in subsection 243(2) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, after June 5, 2011.

Section.Text: Subsection 9(2) of the Canadian Human Rights Act is repealed.
Paragraph 15(1)(c) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 235(2)(b) of the Canada Labour Code is repealed.
Section 15 of the Conflict of Interest Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph (1)(a), for the purpose of maintaining his or her employment opportunities or ability to practice his or her profession on leaving public office, a reporting public office holder may engage in employment or the practice of a profession in order to retain any licensing or professional qualifications or standards of technical proficiency necessary for that purpose if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reporting public office holder does not receive any remuneration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Commissioner is of the opinion that it is not incompatible with the reporting public office holder’s duties as a public office holder.

Section.Text: Sections 165 to 167 come into force one year after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Paragraph 22(2.1)(b) of the Judges Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the four other judges, $232,300 each.

Heading.Label: PART 14
Heading.TitleText: NORDION AND THERATRONICS DIVESTITURE AUTHORIZATION ACT

Section.Text: Despite section 12 of the Nordion and Theratronics Divestiture Authorization Act, section 9 of that Act is deemed to have come into force on April 20, 1993.
The definitions employer and employment in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Pension Plan are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: employer means a person liable to pay salary, wages or other remuneration in relation to employment, and, in relation to an officer, includes the person from whom the officer receives their remuneration;
employment means the state of being employed under an express or implied contract of service or apprenticeship, and includes the tenure of an office;

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 8(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every employee who is employed by an employer in pensionable employment shall, by deduction as provided in this Act from the remuneration in respect of the pensionable employment paid to the employee by the employer, make an employee’s contribution for the year in which the remuneration is paid to the employee of an amount equal to the product obtained when the contribution rate for employees for the year is multiplied by the lesser of

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 9(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every employer shall, in respect of each employee employed by the employer in pensionable employment, make an employer’s contribution for the year in which remuneration in respect of the pensionable employment is paid to the employee of an amount equal to the product obtained when the contribution rate for employers for the year is multiplied by the lesser of

Section.Text: Subsection 21(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Amount to be deducted and remitted by employer

Subsection.Text: Every employer paying remuneration to an employee employed by the employer at any time in pensionable employment shall deduct from that remuneration as or on account of the employee’s contribution for the year in which the remuneration in respect of the pensionable employment is paid to the employee any amount that is determined in accordance with prescribed rules and shall remit that amount, together with any amount that is prescribed with respect to the contribution required to be made by the employer under this Act, to the Receiver General at any time that is prescribed and, if at that prescribed time the employer is a prescribed person, the remittance shall be made to the account of the Receiver General at a financial institution (within the meaning that would be assigned by the definition financial institution in subsection 190(1) of the Income Tax Act if that definition were read without reference to its paragraphs (d) and (e)).

Section.Text: This Part is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2006.
Section 1679 of the Jobs and Economic Growth Act is amended by replacing the subsection 10(4) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply to a chief executive if he or she declares, after giving notice to the Chief Human Resources Officer appointed under subsection 6(2.1) of the Financial Administration Act, that it is not practical to apply those subsections given the size of that portion of the public sector.

Section.Text: Subsection 1680(1) of the Act is amended by replacing the subsections 38.1(1) and (2) that it enacts with the following:

Subsection.Text: Within 60 days after the end of each financial year, each chief executive must prepare and submit to the Chief Human Resources Officer appointed under subsection 6(2.1) of the Financial Administration Act a report for that financial year on the activities, in the portion of the public sector for which the chief executive is responsible, respecting disclosures made under section 12.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Report to President of the Treasury Board — disclosures under section 12
Subsection.Text: Within six months after the end of each financial year, the Chief Human Resources Officer must prepare and submit to the President of the Treasury Board a report for that financial year that provides an overview of the activities, throughout the public sector, respecting disclosures made under section 12.

Section.Text: The Department of Veterans Affairs Act is amended by adding the following after section 1:

For the purposes of this Act, dependant means the spouse or common-law partner or child of any person referred to in subparagraph 4(a)(i), or any other person who provided or received care or support, financial or otherwise, to or from that person.
Paragraph 5(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the care, treatment or other benefits to be provided or that the Minister will pay for in whole or in part, the circumstances in which the Minister will pay in whole or in part and the circumstances in which the Minister may cease to pay in whole or in part;

Section.Text: Subsection 435.01(2) of the Canada Elections Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An allowance fund for a quarter is the product of the number of valid votes cast in the election referred to in subsection (1) multiplied by the applicable following number:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.3825, for the quarter that begins on April 1, 2012 and the three following quarters;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $0.255, for the quarter that begins on April 1, 2013 and the three following quarters; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: $0.1275, for the quarter that begins on April 1, 2014 and the three following quarters.

Section.Text: This Part comes into force on April 1, 2012.
Section 15 of the Special Retirement Arrangements Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who is subject to a retirement compensation arrangement that is designated by the regulations is required to contribute to the Retirement Compensation Arrangements Account, by reservation from the person’s salary, from any benefit that is or becomes payable to, or in respect of, that person or otherwise, in the manner and under the circumstances prescribed in respect of that arrangement, at the rate or rates established by the regulations in respect of that arrangement.

Heading.TitleText: An act to amend certain acts in relation to pensions and to enact the Special Retirement Arrangements Act and the Pension Benefits Division Act

Section.Text: Despite subsection 109(1) of An Act to amend certain Acts in relation to pensions and to enact the Special Retirement Arrangements Act and the Pension Benefits Division Act, chapter 46 of the Statutes of Canada, 1992, (in this section referred to as the “amending Act”) and Order in Council P.C. 1994-2097, made on December 14, 1994 and registered as SI/94-146,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 2(4) and 6(2), sections 8, 11 and 18, subsection 33(2), sections 40 and 41, subsection 48(1) and sections 61, 68 and 70 of the amending Act are deemed to have come into force on December 15, 1994;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 42.1(1)(a) of the Public Service Superannuation Act, as enacted by section 22 of the amending Act, is deemed to have come into force on December 15, 1994;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 50.1(1)(a) of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act, as enacted by section 49 of the amending Act, is deemed to have come into force on December 15, 1994;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 26.1(1)(a) of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act, as enacted by section 77 of the amending Act, is deemed to have come into force on December 15, 1994; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 106 of the amending Act is deemed to have come into force on December 14, 1994.

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Motor Vehicle Safety Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Sections 5 and 6 do not apply to a resident of Canada who imports a vehicle that is licensed in the United States if the vehicle is rented in the United States from a vehicle rental business and is being imported into Canada for non-commercial purposes.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Importer to remove vehicle from Canada
Subsection.Text: A resident of Canada who imports a vehicle under subsection (1.1) shall remove the vehicle from Canada within a period of 30 days, or any other prescribed period, beginning on the day on which the vehicle is imported.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rental business to remove vehicle from Canada
Subsection.Text: If, within the period referred to in subsection (1.2), the resident of Canada delivers the vehicle to a vehicle rental business in Canada with the consent of the business, then the vehicle rental business shall remove the vehicle from Canada before the end of that period.
For greater certainty, if a vehicle rental business is required under subsection (1.3) to remove the vehicle from Canada, subsection (1.2) does not apply to the resident of Canada.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting vehicles imported under subsection (1.1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the importation or removal of vehicles under subsections (1.1) to (1.4); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: defining, for the purposes of those subsections, any term used in them.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 17(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every corporation that contravenes any provision of this Act or the regulations
The portion of subsection 17(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every individual who contravenes any provision of this Act or the regulations

Section.Text: The portion of section 8.4 of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If there is a sales tax harmonization agreement with the government of a province, the appropriate minister may pay to the province out of amounts received in a fiscal year under Part IX of the Excise Tax Act
Section 8.7 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 8.7(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: Payments that were made before the date on which the Keeping Canada’s Economy and Jobs Growing Act was assented to and that could have been authorized under section 8.4, as amended by that Act, if that version of section 8.4 had been in force on the date those payments were made are, for greater certainty, ratified and confirmed and all actions taken in respect of those payments are ratified and confirmed.

Heading.Label: PART 22
Heading.TitleText: DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES AND SKILLS DEVELOPMENT ACT

Section.Text: Subsection 27(2) of the Department of Human Resources and Skills Development Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson and Vice-Chairperson shall reside in the National Capital Region or at the place designated by the Governor in Council under subsection (1), or within reasonable commuting distance of that region or place.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 1
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: LIST OF COUNTRIES AND APPLICABLE TARIFF TREATMENTS

Goods imported by mail or courier for personal or household use.

The following goods are not eligible for classification importation, may be released only in accordance with the accounting provisions of the Customs Act or the applicable regulations on condition that the importer or owner of the goods provides, before the release of the goods, any certificate, licence, permit or other document and any information that is required under any Act of Parliament or any regulation made under such an Act that prohibits, controls or regulates the importation of goods, but this exclusion from eligibility for classification does not apply to any goods imported under the authority of and in accordance with a general import permit issued under subsection 8(1.1) or section 8.3 of the Export and Import Permits Act.
The value of goods imported that are eligible to be classified under this heading shall not exceed $500.

The following goods are not eligible for classification under tariff item No. 9825.10.00, 9825.20.00 or 9825.30.00: alcoholic beverages; tobacco; tobacco products; goods subject to any additional customs duty under sections 21.1 to 22 of this Act; goods subject to any tax imposed under subsection 23(1) of the Excise Tax Act; goods in respect of which tax is not payable under Division III of Part IX of that Act; and goods in respect of which tax is payable under section 212.1 of that Act if an amount must be deducted from that tax under section 214.1 of that Act.

row.entry: -Goods which, if classified in accordance with the provisions of Chapters 1 to 97, would be subject to a rate of customs duty under the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff that is not more than 5 percentage points greater or less than the rate provided by this tariff item under the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff.

-Goods which, if classified in accordance with the provisions of Chapters 1 to 97, would be subject to a rate of customs duty under the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff that is not more than 5 percentage points greater or less than the rate provided by this tariff item under the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff.

-Goods which, if classified in accordance with the provisions of Chapters 1 to 97, would be subject to a rate of customs duty under the Most-Favoured- Nation Tariff that is not more than 5 percentage points greater than the rate provided by this tariff item under the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff.
